stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced May 6, 2026: consolidated PAT Rs 3,662 cr (+103% YoY) - boosted by one-time Rs 1,195 cr fair-value gain from consolidating Bajaj Auto International Holdings AG (KTM parent) effective Nov 18, 2025; consolidated revenue Rs 17,832 cr (+41% YoY, record quarter). Standalone PAT Rs 2,746 cr (+34% YoY), revenue Rs 16,006 cr (+31.8% YoY); record quarterly volumes ~13.71 lakh units. FY26 consolidated PAT Rs 10,744 cr (+47%); revenue Rs 62,905 cr (+23%). Board declared Rs 150/share final dividend (1,500%) and approved tender-route buyback of 46.94 lakh shares (1.68% equity) at Rs 12,000/share for Rs 5,633 cr - largest Bajaj Auto buyback in 4 years; combined payout ~100% of FY26 PAT. Buyback/dividend record date May 29, 2026. Stock hit 52-week high Rs 10,738.40 on May 7 (+4% intraday); ICICI Securities raised target to Rs 12,000. April 2026 wholesales +40% YoY at 5.14 lakh units (domestic +13%, exports +83%).
stocks.sector_context: Indian 2W sector in strong cyclical uptrend in FY26: retail registrations +13.4% YoY and wholesale volumes +10.7%, aided by Sept-2025 GST rate cuts and rural revival. April 2026 retail +13% YoY at 1.91M units. Premium motorcycles +40.7% YoY, scooters +18.5% - mix shift favouring Bajaj's Pulsar refresh. Industry expected to grow high-single-digit in FY27 despite West Asia geopolitical risk to commodity/fuel costs. Marriage and residual festive demand supportive in May.
stocks.peer_comparison: April 2026 wholesale growth: Hero MotoCorp +85.4% (5.66L), Bajaj +40% (5.14L), Eicher/Royal Enfield +30.7% (1.13L), TVS Motor +5.8% (4.55L). Bajaj domestic 2W share has eroded from ~20% (FY10-12) to ~11-12% as TVS climbed to ~18% (18-year high). Bajaj #2 in EV2W (April 2026 Chetak ~32.9k units, +71.7% YoY; ~21% share) behind TVS (~28%) and ahead of Hero (~11%). Premium Pulsar N/NS refresh now >50% of 150cc+ sales; Latin America posted record quarterly volume/revenue. Pre-results Jefferies had downgraded Bajaj to 'Hold' (TP Rs 7,500) and Hero to 'Underperform', kept 'Buy' on TVS (TP Rs 3,225) and 'Accumulate' on Eicher (TP Rs 7,700) - prints since then will likely prompt upward revisions.
red_flags: Consolidated headline PAT (+103%) flattered by one-time Rs 1,195 cr KTM/BAIHAG remeasurement gain - underlying standalone growth (+34%) is the cleaner read
Stock at 52-week high and up ~12% in six sessions - much of Q4/buyback catalyst priced in; thin margin of safety for fresh entries
Q4 margin compression flagged despite record revenue; KTM consolidation may dilute group margin in FY27
Multi-year domestic market-share erosion vs TVS and Royal Enfield in core ICE segments
Pre-results Jefferies 'Hold' with TP Rs 7,500 (well below CMP) signals valuation stretch for some sell-side desks
No ASM/GSM, SEBI, promoter-pledge or auditor concerns identified
catalysts: Buyback + dividend record date May 29, 2026: Rs 12,000 tender price ~15% over CMP creates near-term price floor and arbitrage flows
May 2026 monthly wholesale dispatch print (early June) - momentum check vs record Q4 volumes
Sell-side target-price upgrades catching up to record Q4 and 100% FY26 payout
FY26 AGM and management FY27 volume/margin guidance
Continued KTM/international consolidation and export momentum into FY27
